lessons_learned: Historical baseline must be time-weighted because Miami TMAX distribution has warmed materially across decades.
For early May, the upper bin is not a rare tail; recent analogs near May 5 put >=88F around 37% before using forecast guidance.
A single deterministic max bin is fragile. Output must be a full calibrated probability distribution over bins.
T-12 hour forecasts must ingest live observations and nowcast features, not only model forecast highs.
Forecast errors of 3-4F are large enough to move two bins, so uncertainty must be explicitly widened near bin boundaries.
Market decisions should separate meteorological probability from price/edge/execution timing.

responsibilities: Fetch or accept forecast model guidance snapshots.
Fetch same-day observations, including hourly temperature, dew point, wind, cloud cover, rainfall, and heat-index-relevant variables.
Store all data snapshots with issued_at timestamps to avoid lookahead leakage.

responsibilities.Calendar features: day_of_year, month, seasonal windows, holiday/weekend optional.

responsibilities.Climate features: rolling 10-year normals, same-day analogs, trend-adjusted normals.

responsibilities.Forecast features: NWS/NBM/HRRR/GFS/ECMWF-style high forecasts where available, ensemble spread, forecast deltas.

responsibilities.Observation nowcast features: current temp, morning low, temperature rise rate, dew point, wind direction/speed, cloud/rain flags.

responsibilities.Boundary features: distance from forecast high to each 2F bin boundary.

recommended_approach: Model residual distribution conditional on season, horizon, current observations, and recent forecast bias.
Convert continuous TMAX distribution into bin probabilities by integration over bin thresholds.
Blend with analog climatology using horizon-dependent weights.

responsibilities: Compare fair prices to Kalshi bid/ask/orderbook.
Include transaction fees, spread, and liquidity.
Require margin of safety higher near unstable bin boundaries.

critical_guardrails: Never output only one max bin without probabilities.
Never train and evaluate using random splits across dates; use chronological walk-forward validation.
Never use observations or finalized daily TMAX unavailable at prediction timestamp.
Never allow full-history climatology to dominate recent-season analogs for warm-season Miami.
Always report probability mass on adjacent bins and the upper tail.
Always flag when the top two bins are within 10 percentage points.
Always preserve generated_at, data_cutoff, source, and forecast issue time metadata.
next_iteration_priorities: Add archived forecast snapshots so the model can learn guidance-specific bias by horizon.
Add hourly observation ingestion for T-minus-day nowcasting.
Backtest specifically on early-May Miami cases and >=88F misses.
Calibrate the upper tail so that >=88F is not systematically underweighted.
Build a reproducible CLI that emits one JSON prediction record per event and one backtest report per run.
